leg: stale-union fix ACCEPTANCE (routing-aware guard + incremental top-up, on top of the zero-decode mask)
fix_under_test: axolotl fix/routed-stale-union @ 45d2904 — stage() cover-checks each call's routed union against the staged one and tops up ONLY missing experts into the live resident tensor. Mechanism confirmed by the drift probe (42/42 escapes on layers.0 only, decaying counts, 15/15 steps clean).
prior_layers: bug layer 1 = fill CONTENT (fixed by zero-decode mask dcd9ac2, halved the gap 0.182->0.104); bug layer 2 = STALE UNION at the backward->forward boundary block (this fix). Prediction if these are ALL the layers: gap collapses to ~1x floor.
design: warm-up throwaway + 3 whole arms + 1 routed_fixed (ROUTED=1, DRIFT_LOG=1, STAGEDCNT), Qwen3-30B seq256/150 steps, seed 42, prediction_loss_only — the standard bracket at the decisive point.

criteria.floor: spread of the 3 whole finals (five prior brackets: 0.0010)
criteria.STRONG_PASS: gap <= 1x floor — routed training indistinguishable from whole; graduates to feature/expert-store and the gate OPENS for training
criteria.PASS: gap <= 5x floor — accept with a residual note; graduate with the gate documenting the residual
criteria.FAIL: gap > 5x floor — a third bug layer exists; fix stays on the side branch, gate stays closed
criteria.in_run_mechanism_control: DRIFT lines must appear (block-0-only) — proves the top-up path is live in THIS run; zero drift lines would mean the fix didn't engage (void)

cost_budget: 5 arms ~ $8.5, ~3 h, PRO 6000. ABORT any arm > 90 min.
note_on_expectations: two short-horizon extrapolations already failed in this program (15-step OLMoE, CPU-fake efficacy); this bracket is the only accepted standard.
